entry.title: Re: MDG - Edition Concept
entry.content:
Dear Member

Edition Management Post is Good . very useful.

we are using s4/hana 2022/2023  mdg. while configuring edition i am not able to find any release E1-Release ,E2 -Rlease  E3 -In Process E4 - In process in my Development system..  Mode- Co-Deployed System.  We have executed BC Set (CA-MDG-APP-FIN_EDITION_08) Also. but still the issue is persisting. Can you please give me solution.

Thanks&Regards,

Chiyaan Ravi

entry.title: Re: How to add the bacth number
entry.content:
Hello @heynurhadi 

If the current stock of a material doesn't show batch numbers, it means that the material is not batch managed.

You can't just "add" batch numbers to the current stock. You have to activate batch management for the material, that might prove challenging as several prerequisites have to be met beforehand.

See the notes on when batch management can be switched on/off:

  • 30656 - Change base unit of measure/batch management requirement
  • 1521082 - M3530 Change Material : Batch / Non Batch Managed

The following notes offer an SAP consulting service to set/reset batch management of materials with existing stock and documents:

  • 533276 - Setting the batch management requirement
  • 533383 - Resetting the batch management requirement

I had a chance to use the tool to set batch management. It works flawlessly. I can highly recommend it.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Calculation View Currency conversion with decimal shi...
entry.content: I checked TCURX and it has data. Otherwise the NETWR column would be wrong for the HUF currency. And this is the table with the important data for the decimal shift. HUF has the CURRDEC value of 0 in the table.

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Warsaw 2024 – Key Highlights, Insights, and Community Feedback
entry.content:
Great summary and pics - I see some familiar faces 😊

And it's a crowd! Amazing! Can't remember the exact numbers but it looks your SAP Inside Track increased a lot the last years, right @Bartek ? Wonderful, congratulations! 👏

entry.title: Re: How to Enable Edit Option in Make Company Code Settin...
entry.content:
Hello,

Have you checked the following information?

Make Company Code Settings - Asset Accounting-Specific

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/3781c5d2efea4956b4896d728916ad78.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/8e71a76adeb84940b785936842cc7574.html?locale=en-US

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: 2H2024 - Delegation Available for Performance Reviews
entry.content: Maybe this can be done using the proxy function, worth exploring

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano ,

I am very interested in participating in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. I believe SAP certification would be a valuable asset for my career change.

As someone in the early stages of this transition, I’m eager to learn more about SAP and gain practical skills that will help me make a smooth transition into this new career path. I also fit into several of the underrepresented categories mentioned in the program, as I am a first-generation college student, a women and currently in the process of a mid-career shift.

Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to joining the program and starting my learning journey.

Best regards,
Fatima

entry.title: Re: Application Decisions for HR Time Management
entry.content: Thank you @Michael_Raih , for an interesting article. Can I please ask clarification ... You mention SAP SuccessFactors under 'Rostering and Shift Optimization. We are using SuccessFactors for global time management processes, but our shift population is mostly out of scope as SuccessFactors does not have shift rostering/optimization functionality. Small population have their shifts uploaded to use Time Off and Timesheet in SuccessFactors, but not shift planning. Enhanced Calendar View of employee is being delivered to provide simple shift editor but not shift planning as far as I understand. Can you please advise how is SAP SuccessFactors relevant for rostering and shift optimization? Perhaps we are missing some functionality. Just seeking more understanding. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Difference in Configuration between SAP ALE and SAP EDI
entry.content: I think that ChatGPT or any GenAI will give you good answers.

entry.title: Re: How to integrate SAP Project and Resource Management...
entry.content: Hello Sai Vivek, Thank you very much for the detailed answers and possibilities. I have a follow up question. The SAP KBA article says that one could replicate basic workforce information via APIs https://me.sap.com/notes/2905918. I was thinking we replicate the worker information to S/4 using this approach. Then S/4 can push the worker records to MDI and subsequently to SPRM. Is that an option?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David, 

I am excited to have gotten this opportunity at the right time. I am a 23 year old Business graduate, who identifies as the following categories;

  • Woman
  • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves.

I work as an associate functional consultant at my firm, and am looking for a professional certification to further validate and authenticate my skills and experience. Most people do not get an opportunity like this to get a professional certification, so I am really grateful to have a chance to apply. Looking forward to my learning journey! 

Regards,

Aaisha.

entry.title: Re: The Hidden Gem Using UI Masking to Achieve Compliance
entry.content:
Hi sreehere2020, I suggest having a look into the community topic pages for UI Data Protection: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/ui-data-protection

There's a few deeper "case studies" available in the "interesting to read" section, and the team have provided several dozens of blogs with more details. There was a direct link in the Community page, but this seems to have been removed. You may want to have a look at this filtering of blogs instead: https://community.sap.com/t5/forums/searchpage/tab/message?filter=location&q=%22UI%20masking%22&noSynonym=false&location=category:technology&sort_by=-topicViews&collapse_discussion=true 

Hope this helps!

Tobias

entry.title: Re: Types of CDS Views
entry.content: Lots of confusions, the topics you are talking about are not related. ADT can be used on ABAP NOT Cloud. CDS views are quite simple in their basic form. Consumption views, Interface views and Annotations in CDS Views are names used in ABAP RAP. What are you learning, which tutorials are you doing? Also, there are ABAP CDS views and HANA CDS views. You may look at the ABAP and HANA official documentations. If needed, you may ask ChatGPT or any other GenAI.

entry.title: Re: Error opening Guided Development in VSCode
entry.content: Thanks for your ticket, let us continue the communication there

entry.title: Re: Sorting Alfa Numeric Value. Postion 1.1.1, 1.1.1.20 etc. SQL Anywhere
entry.content: Your sample just shows a lexicographic order, which is the default for char columns. However, do you have cases where 1.1.2 should appear before 1.1.10 - because that would be not that easy to sort, as that would require a "hierarchical sort order" of the separate components rather than a simple lexicographic order?

entry.title: Self Billing Price change
entry.content:
Hello,

I work in an automobile industry and we are currently facing issues with self billing due to price change.

Scenario ;

Sale of car parts

First payment made by customer 19128,73 and second payment for 2822,94. This 2 payments are as a result of a unit price change from 42.08 to 48.29 .

Below screen corresponds to the customers portal showing how much they have paid

Below is the vendors SAP screen whereby we are unable to identify the additional 2822,94 made

Accounts receivable team is unable to do cash allocation as the second payment is not showing in SAP. Please how can this be solved ?

Is there a automatic way of resolving this issue ?

entry.title: Re: exit variable with "E_TH_CHAS"
entry.content: I used NewLineCheckMode: None, cf. Technical Settings and/or Crosstab settings in the Design Panel; you should also check note 2844345.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Elements Object Page Table: Weird row selection behavior
entry.content:
Hi Team, really nobody met this behavior? How does your implementation behave regarding multiselection and actions?

The issue was in the meantime resolved by inline action buttons and switching of the multiselection, but anyway ...

Thanks,

Zdenek

entry.title: Re: External Learning Request (not SF-182 related)
entry.content:
Hi,

we implemented the External Training Request app by Flexso. You can find it in the SAP Store. It does exactly what you expect, and does it well. And we are really satisfied with it. There is also another vendor, have a look in the SAP Store.

Feel free to reach out if you want more info: kunell@pictet.com.

I made a demo of this in a prior SIG call. So it could be possible to find this in replay too.

Best regards,

Kris

entry.title: Can we apply heading style and body text in SAP Enable Now for simulations?
entry.content:
There is a text formatting section in simulation, but it doesn't allow to select heading or body text style.

Where can we apply heading style/body text style to text in demo/practice bubbles or text that is inserted on screen in simulation?











##SAPEnableNow ##SimulationsinEnableNow ##DemoMode ##PracticeMode ##EditingSimulatio

entry.title: Re: Diminishing/Declining Method for Chart of Depreciatio...
entry.content:
Hi Andro,

Yes, we are aware that IFRS allows diminishing depreciation method. However, this is used extremely rarely. Thus we decided not to add such a method to our default pre-delivery content. I recommend to either go for straight-line depreciation (best business practice) or usage of production depreciation method in case it needs to be more detailed.

If you are still convinced to use the diminishing method, you can apply for a custom depreciation key via this process: Verify Configuration for Fit-to-Standard Workshop for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Best regards,

 Mick

entry.title: Re: Types of CDS Views
entry.content:
Hi,

welcome to ABAP in Eclipse. Do you know our Getting Started help content?

In regards to your CDS view question: Does this help content answer your first questions?

Kind regards,

Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Difference in Configuration between SAP ALE and SAP EDI
entry.content:
Hi 

See these threads it may help you 

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/difference-between-ale-and-edi/td-p/2283196

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/configuration-of-edi-and-ale/m-p/2481013

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: How to Set Up the Email Notification for Flexible Workflow in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Unfortunately I am not familiar with that solution (flexible workflow scenario) @Adhil, and as far as I can tell you haven't provided enough information for someone who isn't familiar with the scenario to have an opinion on what the problem could be.

Instead of commenting on this blog you should explain your problem properly and create your own post (question) where people can comment, ask for more detail, and suggest a solution.

Questions are like bug reports, if you don't provide enough information for people to understand what you try to do, what you have done, what you expect and what you got that wasn't as expected (personally I prefer that people also provide information about what they have already done themselves to try and figure out what the problem is), you can't expect people to be able to provide good suggestions on how your problem can be solved (or in some cases tell you that your expectations are the problem and everything works as designed).

entry.title: UD Reversal Note - IM and EWM SLOC
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We need to carryout UD reversal in certain scenarios for stocks taken in IM and EWM locations. Now we are struggling a lot in case of trying to reverse the Material Documents. 

We are in SAP S4 2020 (On Premise).


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: UD Reversal Note - IM and EWM SLOC
entry.content:
Hi,

thank you for the details provided.

Please read following KBAs:

- for IM scenario:

3022268 - How to reverse usage decision and its stock postings?

- for EWM scenario:

3352129 - Reverse usage decision stock posting for EWM inspection lot (origin: 17)

There are no alternative methods available.

Have a nice day,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Self Billing Price change
entry.content: Did you filter only uncleared items, try to display also cleared item for this customer?

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Public: OData v4 REST API and purchasing history.
entry.content:
Hi All,

Maybe somebody knows an answer.
I have CDS view, created in Fiori, with data source "I_PurchaseOrderHistoryAPI01" and its field "PurchasingHistoryDocument".

I'm searching in documentation, how  to reach to this field via pure REST API. For example I can get PO with following request:
<my tenant>.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurchaseOrder"
or I can get PO items and so on. It works well. PO API is described here, for instance: https://api.sap.com/api/CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001/resource/Purchase_Order

Could anybody tell me, please, what API item should I use to get "PurchasingHistoryDocument"?

entry.title: Tutorial Design and Deploy Your First Integration Flow Error Connect request reply to receiver
entry.content:
At link tutorial: Design and Deploy Your First Integration Flow | SAP Tutorials
When I followed Step 6.4, in the Address field, I entered: https://refapps-espm-ui-cf.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/espm-cloud-web/espm.svc

However, I encountered the error shown in the image below.

To resolve this issue, I suggest using the following URL instead: https://refapp-espm-ui-cf.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/espm-cloud-web/espm.svc/

SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Can we fix the player bar location at bottom in SAP Enable Now and remove the toggle button?
entry.content:
The player bar is easy to toggle from top to bottom, but can we allow it to be fixed at bottom location? Where can we do these changes?

#SAPEnableNow #SimulationEditinginEnableNow

entry.title: Re: New 2H 2023 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
Hello @Volker_Ruof 

If we have a client who wants a semi-monthly timesheet (from 1 to 15 and from 16 to 30), how is it possible to do this? Is it possible through the Time Recording Calendar? And if the 15th is in the middle of a week, how does the approval of that week work?

Thanks

entry.title: SAP User Experience News for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Q4/2023
entry.content:
Joule is now generally available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023. Joule saves you a lot of time when you need to refer to documentation, by giving you a concise summary and pointing you to relevant sections. It also helps you find the right application for well over one hundred navigational scenarios, as well as providing some transactional interactions for selected business objects. If you move up to Feature Pack Stack 2 (FPS2), you can also benefit from built-in collaboration, with the SAP Collaboration Manager now generally available with enhanced functionality. Talking about collaboration, FPS2 also enables users to initiate a chat or call directly from a contact card via Microsoft Teams integration.

This post covers:

  • Joule
  • New collaboration capabilities via:
    -  SAP Collaboration Manager
    -  Contact card integration with Microsoft Teams
  • SAP Mobile Start update
  • News for UI developers, web and mobile

If you want to see how good SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition now looks, across all the main product areas, then I thoroughly recommend that you have a look at the new online Product Tour.

In case you haven’t already seen these, do have a look at the user experience innovations which came with FPS00 and FPS01:

  • SAP User Experience Update: What’s New for SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)
  • User Experience Advances with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)

Joule

Users can save around ten minutes every time they need to refer to the documentation, by using Joule to give them a concise summary, created using generative AI. The response also provides three links to the most relevant sections in the documentation. We call this the Informational Pattern. Figure 1 below shows an example for someone working in sales, and Figure 2 shows an example for someone working in procurement.

Figure 1: Using Joule in sales to get help from the documentation, in this case asking “How can I make mass changes to sales orders?”. Alt Text: An image showing Joule on the left, with the query text and an eleven line response below it, followed by the option to expand to see the search results. On the right, an image of Joule is shown with the search results expanded to show three results, the first one called “Mass Changes of Sales Documents”, referring to the documentation of the app with that name. Each search result has a heading with three lines of explanatory text below it.

 

 

 

Figure 2: Using Joule in procurement to get help from the documentation, in this case asking “Can I configure a workflow for my purchase requisitions?”. Alt Text: An image showing Joule on the left, with the query text and a nine line response below it, followed by the option to expand to see the search results. On the right, an image of Joule is shown with the search results expanded to show three results, the first one called “How to Configure the Flexible Workflow for Purc…”, referring to the documentation. Each search result has a heading with three lines of explanatory text below it.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023, users can also benefit from the navigational pattern for over one hundred use cases, in areas such as procurement, finance, sales, and service management. This pattern allows them to navigate directly to apps relevant to their query. You can find out more in this blog post:

  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

 Furthermore, we provide some selected transactional use cases in these areas too.

 To find out more about Joule, have a look at the documentation:

  • Joule in SAP Help Portal

Planned Innovations

As announced at SAP TechEd on October 8th, we have many plans for making Joule even more useful for users, as well as enabling you to build your own skills to extend what SAP delivers. As always, our plans are subject to change, but of course we hope to deliver as planned.

Some highlights are:

  • Joule for Mobile, built into SAP Mobile Start. As announced, this is planned to become available in November 2024.
  • Joule studio in SAP Build for building your own skills is planned to become available for beta testing later this year, and become generally available in Q1/2025. Get a preview by watching the recording of this TechEd talk:
    -  AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Beyond that, we are working on having many Joule agents interact with each other, across our products – making Joule agentic. Also, to support hands-free interaction, we are working on integration with Siri, so that users can use voice to interact.

 If you haven’t watched it live, then have a look at the replay of the TechEd talk by our AI leaders:

  • AI100 | Artificial intelligence that creates real-world results

New Collaboration Capabilities

With Feature Pack Stack 2 we introduce the following new collaboration capabilities.

SAP Collaboration Manager

SAP Collaboration Manager allows users to:

  • Take notes when using an application; these notes are pinned to the application, and are listed in the Relevant Chats tab when a user has that application open in the same window as the SAP Collaboration Manager.
  • Ad-hoc chat with colleagues, directly in the SAP system.
  • Share and annotate screenshots; a link is provided to the recipient with which they can navigate directly to the UI where the screenshot was taken.
  • Share the business context.
  • Upload and share files.

Figure 3 shows an example where a user Jones Davidson has created a chat with a colleague Alyssa Arnold and shared a screenshot along with a request to her to take a look at the two open sales orders in the screenshot.

For a closer look at some of the above features, watch it in action here in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editions 2308 system (what is shown is now available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02):

  • Video: SAP Collaboration Manager (2:10 minutes)

Figure 3: Using SAP Collaboration Manager to chat with a colleague and share a screenshot Alt Text: An image showing a list of three sales orders selected in the Manage Sales Orders app, and SAP Collaboration Manager as an overlay on the right showing the chat text and the screenshot image.

Beyond the above features, users can now also:

  • Mark their favorites, and filter by them (using the Favorites tab).
  • Set chats to unread.
  • Add and share insight cards from My Home.

These features are shown in Figure 4.

Figure 4: On the left: SAP Collaboration Manager offers tabs for “Recent chats”, “All Chats” and “Favorites”. You see the star indicating a favorite; the blue bar on the left of the second chat indicates that it has been set to unread. On the right: an insight card added to a chat. Alt Text: An image showing two screenshots of SAP Collaboration Manager. On the left, showing “All Chats” tab with a list of three chats. The second entry in the list is marked as unread, and has a star indicating it is a favorite. On the right, showing an insight card with a vertical bar chart embedded in the chat.

Contact card integration with Microsoft Teams

Users can now initiate a chat or call with Microsoft Teams directly from a contact card in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02. This feature is also available for SAP S/4HANA 2023 (on-premise) with FPS02.

Figure 5 shows two examples for this: using it directly on the My Home page to contact the creator of a task, and using it to contact a customer directly out of a sales order.

Figure 5: Two examples for how you can initiate a chat or call from a contact card via Microsoft Teams – from a To-Do card in My Home (upper image) and from a sold-to party link (lower image). Alt Text: An image showing two screenshots of popovers next to links referring to contacts -  for the ToDo card the contact is the creator of the task, for the other one it is the sold-to-party of the sales order. Each popover contains a section for initiating a Microsoft Teams collaboration with icons for chat, audio call and video call.

SAP Mobile Start

Users like to feel in charge, and a good way to support that is to let them define their favorites. The My Home page provides this feature and, as we saw above, the SAP Collaboration Manager has introduced this capability too.

Now also SAP Mobile Start lets users very easily define up to six favorites, two of which appear on the start screen next to a “See all” link to show all six. Figure 6 shows you what this looks like.

Figure 6: SAP Mobile Start lets users define their favorite apps / tiles. Alt Text: Three screenshots of SAP Mobile Start are shown. The one on the left shows the start page, which now has a “Favorites” section at the top, above the “Latest To-Dos” and “Insights” sections. The Favorites section shows two apps, each with a KPI shown as a number; on the right it has a “See all” link. The image in the middle shows all the favorites, in this case three are listed. The image on the right shows a list of all apps which can be selected as a favorite.

Find out more about this, as well as the new capability to upload and download attachments from tasks in this blog post:

  • SAP Mobile Start V1.12 - Release Update

News for UI Developers, Web and Mobile

The major news for UI developers for both web and mobile is that SAP Build now supports ABAP Cloud, and not just CAP (Cloud Application Programming model) for other programming languages such as Java and JavaScript. Get a comprehensive overview in this blog post from Michael Ameling:

  • Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud!

Providing Joule skills is also a powerful way to enhance the user experience – I covered our main planned innovations there in already in the “Planned Innovations” part of the above Joule section.

News for Web Developers

Alt Text: Image of the white UI5 logo on a blue background with the text “UI5 linter 1.0 New Release” below it.

The main news is that UI5 linter 1.0 has now been released and is generally available. It is a static code analysis tool which helps ensure that you have good quality code, and which saves you a lot of time debugging later by catching issues up front. It can check for problems such as the use of deprecated UI5 libraries, framework APIs etc. as well as the usage of global variables and possible CSP violations. Find out more in this blot post:

  • Release of UI5 linter v1.0

I would also like to point you to the updated SAP BTP Developer’s Guide, where we have now brought in user experience design advice, as well as restructuring it to cover Explore, Discover, Design, Deliver and Scale & Run phases:

  • SAP BTP Developer’s Guide
  • If you read the guide, do spend a few minutes to give us feedback, so that we can improve it even further, by taking this survey to help us improve the guide!

The developer’s guide recommends using SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements, built on OData services, for developing UIs. Should you not be able to follow this recommendation because you have existing applications using different protocols or frameworks, we have options to help you nevertheless:

  • If you have existing back-end code which cannot provide OData services, we have now released the SAPUI5 meta-data controls library sap.ui.mdc, as outlined here:
    -  Smart choices for your UI: When to use sap.ui.mdc
  • If you already have applications written with a different UI technology, such as Angular or React, we have released Version 2.0 of our UI5 Web Components:
    -  Blast Off! UI5 Web Components 2.0 is Now Live!

For friends of ABAP, we recently introduced the ability to create Fiori elements apps directly from within the ABAP Development Tools (ADT). Find out more here:

  • How to create a SAP Fiori Elements application from within ADT?
  • SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release adds closer integration with ABAP Development Tools

News for Mobile Developers

The main news for mobile developers is the latest releases of the mobile SDKs, as well as the integration of the Mobile Development Kit into SAP Build Code. Here are links to find out more:

  • How to Develop SAP MDK Applications in SAP Build Code
  • Streamline SAP MDK Development with the Power of Generative AI and Joule
  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS 24.8.0 is now available
  • SAP BTP SDK for Android 24.8.0 is now available

For a deeper dive, watch the recordings of the Devtoberfest sessions on developing mobile apps:

  • Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK (50:40 minutes)
  • Craft Enterprise Apps for iOS, iPadOS, and visionOS with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code (47:16 minutes)
  • Build Elegant Enterprise Apps for Android with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code (45:24 minutes)

Final Words

I hope you are as excited about these new user experience innovations as I am! FPS02 brings quite a few additional new features beyond user experience, discover all of them here:

  • What’s New Viewer for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02

If you like what you see here, we encourage you to upgrade to FPS02, so that you can run your business even better with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023.

entry.title: FBL5N - The SAP System doesn't automatically fill in the relevant data, by creating a dispute case
entry.content:
Hello together,

we hope, you can help us 😞

Following:

When we create a new dispute case about the transaction FBL5N, then the SAP System doesn't fill in automatically the relevant data, for example: external reference number, the processor etc.

After that we saved the dispute case.

Then, we go to the transaction code UDM_DISPUTE:

When we EDIT and SAVE the dispute case, then the SAP System pull automatically the data, what i don't understand 😞 the right processor, the right external reference number

We work with two BADI's and these BADI's also activated:

  • XXXX_DM_DEF_PROC
  • XXXX_DM_BEFORE_SAVE

Can anynone help us, how we can solve this problem?

Thank you so much for your support.

Best regards.

L. E.

entry.title: Down Payment Processing (Advances from customers) for Service Orders and Service Contracts
entry.content:
Hi Expert !

I want to process Down Payment Processing(advance from customers) on Service Orders and Service Contracts (S/4 HANA Public Cloud).
(I want to process Down Payment Processing for Service Contracts and Service Orders in the same way as SD Sales Orders.)

The process is as follows:
① After creating and releasing the Service Order, create the Down Payment Processing.
② After creating a Service Confirmation, record the accounts receivable (revenue) by Billing Document.

The questions are as follows:
1).Is there functionality to process Down Payment Processing from Service Contracts and Service Orders?
2).Are there plans to release features like 1) above in the future?

Soma

entry.title: Re: Cost of Goods Sold Report
entry.content: Hello. The same request we got. Is there any solution? We need to show COGS for every stage of production: quantity, GL, amounts.

entry.title: EML- READ Entity using partial key fields
entry.content:
Hi Experts,


I have a requirement to read the BO entity using partial key. I have 3 key fields in the BO and want to read the data using only the first two key fields. When I pass only two fields to the READ statement it is not fetching any value. The third key is a numeric type and it will be defaulted as "0" when no data is passed. Is there any way to overcome this issue?

The pseudo code is below. Any help would be appreciated!

 

READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE <Root_entity>

ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( %key-field1 = value1

%key-field2 = value2

"%key-field3 = <No value is passed here> -- This is an integer type

) )

RESULT DATA(lt_result)

FAILED DATA(lt_failed).

entry.title: Re: Handling additional costs on PO-Invoices through OpenText VIM
entry.content:
Hello,

good topic. Have one question - is it possible to post unplanned goods not to G/L account but directly on material master? like it is possible in MIRO.

thanks

entry.title: Can we change the cursor to hand cursor in SAP Enable Now Simulations?
entry.content:
Checked enabling the Cursor Hand option available in EN but it still displays as mouse instead of the desired hand cursor.

How can we add the cursor hand option to demo and practice mode simulations?



#DemoMode #PracticeMode #SimulationEditing #EnableNow #CursorHand

entry.title: Call rest service from SAP API management javascript policy
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

I am looking for an example where in API management a rest service is called in a javascript policy.
I've tried all number of things but nothing works.
Methods i've tried include axios, httpClient, fetch, xmlhttprequest.

Also not sure if the javascript can be extended with node libraries, probably not.

Thanks,

Tom

entry.title: Re: FSM Android: Templates for offline HTML reports not s...
entry.content:
Hi all,

In the meantime, I contacted the SAP support and they provide the solution to me.

Here are the settings needed for displaying the offline reports on Android devices:

  • Activation of company setting CoreSystems.Reports.PreviewAsHTML
  • Deactivation of UI permission CHECKOUT_WITHOUT_REPORT_ENABLED in DTO ServiceCheckout
  • Change of permissions in DTO ReportTemplate: Create => OWN, Read => All
  • Workflow step must be named Report and the related screen type must be Report

Afterwards, it was working for Android as well.

Best regards,

Christoph

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

I hope this message finds you well. I am excited to express my interest in the Digital Skills Initiative, particularly with the focus on learning opportunities for women looking to enhance their skills. With several years of experience in SAP ECC, I recognize the importance of staying current in our rapidly evolving industry. I am eager to upskill myself in both SAP S4 HANA and SAP Fiori, and I plan to complete the respective certifications to further my expertise.

I believe that this initiative not only empowers women but also fosters a supportive community for learning and growth. I am grateful for your efforts in organizing such a valuable program. Thank you for creating opportunities that encourage women to thrive in the tech field. I look forward to the possibility of participating and contributing to this initiative.

Warm regards,
Richa Katiyar

entry.title: Re: what activities are required from SD end merge one pl...
entry.content:
Firstly, have you thought of some activities and have specific questions on those? 

Coming to your question, there may be a little activities needed to do or a lot of activities may be needed... It can also call for a Project in itself. 

I would first start with checking the Open Orders and Deliveries under the Plant that is going to be merged and ask Business on what is planned for these document? Will they be closed or migrated to another Plant..

entry.title: Can you change the highlights style to static highlights in Enable Now simulations?
entry.content:
1. By default, the highlights in Enable Now simulations flicker when it appears in preview/published mode. Can we change this to static highlights? 
  2. Usually, the area outside of focus is faded and the highlight stands out. Can we also remove the spotlight effect and make the entire screen look same?

#SimulationEditing #SAPEnableNow #WorkArea #Producer

entry.title: How to solve simple requirement with RAP, Fiori Elements and FPM
entry.content:
Dear RAP / Fiori Elements / FPM Developers

I am lost, please help me find a solution or clues how i can develop this simple App lined out to you below with Standard Tools like RAP, Fiori Elements and FPM.

The essence of the app is: A List-Object App where the List displays a table of Employees and the Object Page shows the Documents for a selected Employee. Documents can be uploaded, changed, deleted. Employees can NOT be created, updated or deleted.

Leading Entity: CDS_LIST_OF_EMPLOYEES
No Behaviour Definition, it is not allowed to change or create Employees. Data from multiple Standard SAP Tables PAxxxx

Composition: CDS_LIST_OF_DOCUMENTS  (1 Employee has * Documents)
With Behaviour Definition: Upload Document, Delete and Change allowed. Data is in a Z-Table.

Sounds simple? 
I tried out now so many ways, always within the boundaries of RAP, Fiori Elements and FPM (Flexible Programming Model). I went from a pure Fiori Elements App to a Custom Page and Building Blocks from FPM....to no avail. It always felt like I scratched the boundaries of these technologies...with this simple requirement!

For me the best guiding tutorial was from Marius Freitag / Devtoberfest:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SMc1ZzKAWW4 

Yet I couldn't get my app running after hours of "translating" the offered solutions in this video to my specific requirements.

Also glad if you could share your experience with these technologies in REAL LIFE environment (within SAP Modules) and not these vanilla cases of the tutorials.

Thanks so much for your input.
Kind Regards
Paul

entry.title: Re: CAP App not working in BTP Cockpit
entry.content:
Hello Sakthive,

As mentioned above, this is expected behaviour. But there is a solution. In your package.json file, under "cds" section you need to add the following code.

    "fiori": {
      "preview": true
    },
    "server": {
      "index": true
    },
 
This solved my problem. Hope it solves yours too.

entry.title: Re: How to integrate SAP Project and Resource Management...
entry.content:
Hi Aavantika,

Once you have the basic worker information ( workforce person & employment information) in S/4HANA Cloud, which is brought from 3rd party system. S/4HC already has integration of worker data to SPRM. This is via pre-built integration between S/4HC and SPRM using MDI service.

But please remember that integration is already provided by SAP as part of the 6JO scope.

Regards,

Sai

entry.title: Re: CAP- RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded
entry.content: Hi @Diphnesh, I solved it in a different way, since I was using the latest version. My problem was that the querry I was sending to my hana was to large. Therefore I created chunks and send it bit by bit. Still thank you for your answer

entry.title: BYD2405 Cannot assign logon certificate
entry.content:
Hi All,

 

I cannot assign a logon certificate to my account by following the instructions.

Here is the steps I taken:
1. Go to Application and User Management -->Common Tasks--> Edit Trust Certificate Trust List
2. Update CA certificate of user certificate
3. Go to My Settings --> Manage Certificates
4. Click on the "Assign Logon Certificate", then, I got the "No Certificate Available" page

I do have a valid certificate in my browser. 

Best Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2405 Cannot assign logon certificate
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Please refer and follow below two KBAs. They show how to assign login certificate.

3351213 - How to Download and Setup Certificate-Based Logon - SAP for Me

2853767 - Assign Logon Certificate - SAP for Me

It seems that the steps you taken is correct. But there is one point need to be noted when assigning login certificate. ByD doesn't recognize all the issuer of certificates. A valid certificate should be from a trusted certificate authority. Trusted certificate authority can be found in Logon Using Client Certificate (X.509) | SAP Help Portal.

If you cannot get a certificate from a trusted certificate authority, you can request a certificate from SAP Cloud solution.

 

Best Regards,

SAP Technical Support

Cariford

entry.title: T-code for clearing Gl account
entry.content: I want to know all the T-codes used for clearing GL accounts. I have encountered an issue where some GL accounts have no open items, but I still want to clear them. What can I do?

entry.title: External Candidate Authentication Using SAP Custome Data Cloud - Not available for EU Data Centers?
entry.content:
External Candidate Authentication Using SAP Custome Data Cloud - 

Is this feature really not available for customes with EU Data Center Access? What about DC in Switzerland?

When will this be available for EU?

entry.title: Reassigning tasks in Onboarding (2.0)
entry.content:
In ONB 1.0 it was possible for the Recruitment admins to reassign tasks (not delegate, but reassign).  That option does not seem possible in ONB2.0 and it is causing issues where the manager is not completing tasks (absence or apathy!) and causing a bottleneck in the process.

Has anyone found a workaround for this?

Thanks

Emily

Unfortunately, I receive an error from my Partner Demo tenant: "403 Forbidden The request has been blocked by UCON."
What can be source of the error?

entry.title: cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download - separator firlds
entry.content:
Hi.

I have an internal table to be download to txt file with the fields separated by '|'

CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download
  EXPORTING
    filename                   = lv_name
    filetype                  = 'ASC'
    write_field_separator     = '|'
    TRUNC_TRAILING_BLANKS_EOL = 'X'
    TRUNC_TRAILING_BLANKS     = 'X'
  changing
    data_tab                  = t_final.

 It saves the file, but with tabs between the fields (instead of the '|' separator) 

here is the result

Any ideas? What I'm doing wrong?

entry.title: Re: Introduction of SAC Multi-actions Public API
entry.content:
Hi @manu_xu ,

somehow the redirect uri is now a mandatory entry when creating a new oauth client ( interactive usage ) in SAC. 

Do you have a workaround for that?

Thank you and best regards,

Hannes

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Learning System options for practice
entry.content:
Hi @Natalia_A , 

Good question. The Learning Journey "Exploring the SAP SuccessFactors Platform" corresponds to the consultant training which is the first step to embark on your SAP SuccessFactors Certification journey. The course code for this Learning Journey is THR80. 

Therefore, the SAP Learning System Access you should enroll to is the Introduction to SAP SuccessFactors Academy (THR80), which will include access to Front End and Back End (Provisioning). The other system (HR800) is related to the Platform Administration training which is targeted to HR administrators and does not include Provisioning access. 

Hope this will help. Please do not hesitate to comment again on this post if you need further assistance on this topic, 

Best regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Consume ap.365 Service in BW4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Friends 

I tried to consume service from SE80 in BW4HANA.

https://api.365.systems/odata/v2/xxxx

it gets error message:

Incorrect value: Entity <<document>>(0 /0 ). only whitespace allowed; ignoring characters

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Flexible Workflow Extending Email Notification
entry.content:
Hi everyone

I need to extend email for Purchase Order Flexible Workflow. There is very limited data that can be taken and showed into email.

 

I read about limited possibility of extending via Custom Fields ( Custom Data Source ).

Extending data source of mail template for purchas... - SAP Community

I think that the last option could be modify the CDS C_WORKFLOWTASK. What do you think ?

entry.title: Uncover the Power of SAP Commerce Workflow Management with Our Tutorial Video
entry.content:
Playing a video tutorial in this digital era is like turning the pages of a digital playbook - every progressing minute unfolds a new principle, a new lesson learned. Gain in-depth insights into the SAP Commerce workflow extension's features, components, configuration, and practical functionalities with our explainer video.

To explore these features in detail, catch our tutorial at Click to watch the Video  (https://microlearning.opensap.com/media/Recapping+Workflow+Management+for+Workflow+Automated+Actions+in+SAP+Commerce+Cloud/1_tb46ca66)

Sharpen your understanding of human- and system-based workflow design, assignment of tasks to specific Users or User Groups, task-relevant item attachment to a workflow, and so much more! You'll dive deeper into understanding SAP Commerce Cloud and its wide breadth of applications.

Join us on this journey of step-by-step workflow management in Backoffice, in-depth overview of workflow components, live examples, glimpses into actual workflow creation, and how you can automate crucial steps in your workflow. Supplement your knowledge, enhance your skills, and master the art of using SAP Commerce Cloud's Backoffice Workflow Management!

Stay on the cutting edge of learning with our resourceful video tutorial and unlock the full potential of SAP Commerce Cloud Workflow Management in your organization. We're excited for you to glean rich insights - see you on the other side!

entry.title: SSAM 2405 with Dynamic forms - Forms are not loading
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to enable and load the Dynamic forms to SSAM 2405 Mobile application with SAP ERP.

I followed the steps provided in the SAP help portal and configured the Dynamic forms at each level. (Form Explorer, Config panel Form Agent, Form and Template management in the BTP Dynamic forms application).

I can create the forms against the Equipment, Functional Location Object type at Form Explorer and I can see the created forms against the Equipment in the Form Explorer, but the same forms are not loading to the SSAM 2405 application. 

My User Id have all the authorizations in SAP and also in cloud.

Could you please suggest me on downloading the Dynamic forms in SSAM 2405 app?

Thanks!

Regards,

Pramila Vunnam

entry.title: Re: Don't "let it crash", is this the new CAP guideline?
entry.content:
I really like that the new odata adapter doesn't crash the server on some unhandled errors. The old adapter would work fine with uncaught exceptions but would break the server on errors like `Cannot read properties of undefined`. Now all errors seem to be handled properly.
Really glad this was fixed.

PS: new parser and new adapter are not the same. They are different things controlled with two different configs: `odata_new_parser` and `odata_new_adapter`.

entry.title: Re: Sales BOM
entry.content: Adding tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: SAP Learning Introduction
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

My name is Kien, and I’m thrilled to join this SAP Learning Community! I have extensive experience working with SAP, particularly in the FI-CO module. My journey in IT has given me a solid foundation in systems analysis and software development, which has deepened my appreciation for the complexities and capabilities of enterprise resource planning systems.

I am currently working towards obtaining the C_FIORD_2404 - SAP Certified Associate – SAP Fiori Application Developer certification. I am eager to deepen my understanding of SAP Fiori and apply it more effectively in my work. I believe this certification will help me expand my career and contribute to meaningful projects, especially in under-resourced communities like my own.

I am excited about the opportunity to learn from industry experts and collaborate with peers who share my enthusiasm for technology and its transformative potential. I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in technology.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

entry.title: Re: REA1 updated in Analysis tab but not in under the con...
entry.content:
Thanks Victor for your quick responce.

Some how i dig the issue & found that , even the sales order/Billing was created in the past before condition contract creation with the customer , material Etc If the same field combination match in with Business volume selection cretiria in existing CC # , Then in that case that CC # number showing in analysis  pricing . This is not working the way our list price like PR00 or any other standard price condition.

Is there a way restrict to show the CC #number in Analysis if the sales order or billing created before CC# Creation ? becasue CC # Which are determined in condition tab and Analsis Pricing totally different.

Please guide .Thank you !

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Monthly Webinar - October 2024
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform-as-a-service (iPaaS) that enables the design, development, execution, governance, curation, analysis, and management of the full lifecycle of integration flows, APIs, and high-volume business events that connect on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, and data across organizations. SAP Integration Suite is available on multiple cloud providers.

In this webinar, we'll have two sections:

Product Updates: Get updates on key capabilities and latest enhancements released for SAP Integration Suite.


OR

#AskAnythingIntegration: Our panel of experts will answer any questions you have on integration.

Whether I put the input help or a value table behind the data element does not matter. The F4 help does not work.

Is there a solution ?

Or has somebody a similar Function where F4 help works?

Best regards


Maxi

entry.title: How to test sap btp api without passing the cookies?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm trying to test my SAP RAP application in Postman which works well in the browser but cannot get the results in Postman.

I have done the authorization referring to this blog: Manually Testing SAP BTP ABAP Environment APIs with Postman using OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant


In Postman the status shows '200' still getting no results in the body: 


But it works when I copy the cookies from the browser and add them to the request header without even passing any credentials or authorization details.


Is there any way this could work without passing the cookies?

entry.title: is there any possibility to assign the depreciation to the sales division wise
entry.content:
is there any possibility to assign the depreciation to the sales division wise

i am giving here the example for better understanding 

Division is ice-cream for this we are maintaining the whole sales as the distribution channels for transporting of the that ice cream. we are using  the one asset for transport of ice cream for that asset doing depreciation. how we can assign this depreciation to the division. please give the process where i need to enter the division 

Thanking you

entry.title: Re: Cancelpick EWM error message code is /SCDL/ICC001
entry.content:
Hi

These kind of error is resolved by implementing correction instruction. see the notes 

Symptom

You want to cancel picking for an Outbound Delivery Order (ODO). When you try to release the stock assignment, the system raises the message 'Field Batch Number is a required field for the action SAVE' (/SCDL/ICC 001). Then it aborts the app 'Cancel Picking' (/SCWM/CANCPICK).

Other Terms

/SCDL/ICC001, BATCHNO_I, /SCWM/CANCPICK, PDO

Reason and Prerequisites

Program error

The error occurs when the product is batch managed and the picking was done with a single warehouse task, so that there was no batch split.

Solution

Implement the correction instruction

 

See the notes 3455778 - ICC Prevents Cancel Picking for Batch Managed Product

 

But I would suggest to take advise from SAP support team before you implement the note

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Multiple Address Handling: How Address Determination Works in SD Documents
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA cloud, multiple address handling allows you to maintain multiple addresses for one single business partner. When multiple address handling is active, you can use the address selection function in apps for creating and changing sales and distribution (SD) documents to select customer addresses for specific partner functions.

In this blog, we will give examples of how the address determination works in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Prerequisite: Enable Multiple Address Handling in Your Configuration Environment:

The prerequisite to use the multiple address handling capability is to enable below configurations in your configuration environment.

Active multiple address handling in the following configuration activities (for example, in the Manage Your Solution app):

  1. Activate Multiple Address Handling for Customer Master Data in SAP BP (SSCUI: 105612)
  2. Activate Multiple Address Handling in SD Documents Using SAP BP (SSCUI: 105611)

 

Example 1:

Create new addresses

You can create addresses for customers in the Manage Customer Master Data, Manage Business Partner Master Data, or Maintain Business Partner app. For example, a customer has 4 addresses, and all of them are assigned to usage “SHIP-TO (Delivery Address)”.

Check the configuration of partner determination

Display address determination configuration in the configuration activity “Set up Partner Determination of Sales Document Header” (SSCUI 101288):

Partner function

Address determination for partner function

Sold-to party

BBP007 (Receive sales order)

Ship-to party

BBP003 (Send goods)

Bill-to party

BBP004 (Send bill)

Create sales quotation

You are creating a sales quotation.

No address is assigned to the “order-sending address of customer” (mapped to BBP007) in the customer master. The system searches the address overview (general addresses) for valid addresses. There are 4 addresses for sold-to party.

Four addresses have been assigned to the “delivery address” (mapped to BBP003) in customer master. The system searches for delivery addresses valid between the current system date and Dec 31, 9999. There are 4 addresses for ship-to party.

No address is assigned to the “billing address” (mapped to BBP004)  in the customer master. The system searches the address overview (general addresses) for valid addresses. So, the 4 addresses also can be used in bill-to party.

Example 2:

Change the configuration of partner determination

If you don’t want multiple addresses available for sold-to party and bill-to party (meaning only use standard address), you can change the address determination configuration in the configuration activity Set up Partner Determination for Sales Document Header (SSCUI 101288) like below.

Partner function

Address determination for partner function

Sold-to party

XXDFLT (Standard)

Ship-to party

BBP003 (Send goods)

Bill-to party

XXDFLT (Standard)

Create sales quotation:

You are creating a sales quotation.

Only ship-to party has pop-up window for address selection. Four addresses have been assigned to the “delivery address” (mapped to BBP003) in customer master. The system searches for delivery addresses valid between the current system date (for example Sep 11, 2024) and Dec 31, 9999. There are 4 addresses for ship-to party.

 

Sold-to party and bill-to party use the standard address, and only the standard address is available.

Example 3:

Create new addresses

The following customer has 5 addresses. One of the addresses is out of date in address overview. 3 addresses are assigned to usage “Delivery Address”. One address is assigned to “Billing Address”.

Change the configuration of partner determination

Display address determination configuration in the configuration activity “Set up Partner Determination of Sales Document Header” (SSCUI 101288):

Partner function

Address determination for partner function

Sold-to party

BBP007 (Receive sales order)

Ship-to party

BBP003 (Send goods)

Bill-to party

BBP004 (Send bill)

Create sales quotation

You are creating a sales quotation.

No address is assigned to the “order-sending address of customer” (mapped to BBP007) in the customer master. The system searches the address overview (general addresses) for valid addresses. There are 4 addresses for sold-to party.

Three addresses have been assigned to the “delivery address” (mapped to BBP003) in customer master. The system searches for delivery addresses valid between the current system date (for example Sep 11, 2024) and Dec 31, 9999. There are 2 addresses for ship-to party (one address out of date).

One address has been assigned to the “billing address” (mapped to BBP004) in customer master. The system searches for billing addresses valid between the current system date (for example Sep 11, 2024) and Dec 31, 9999. There is only one address for bill-to party.

For more information you can refer to

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/3ca897ae1c384861ab8df820af8859e4.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/d5af44c1bbd74df8b01946486b807984.html

entry.title: Determine if there is data in past 24 months of Actuals
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a business requirement to flag if there is no longer data for the past 24 months in Actuals Key Figure. This is to flag if the record is still active or inactive. To achieve this, my idea is to bring the sum of the past 24 months into the current month of a new key figure. This can be done I think by following SAP Note 2240170. However, I am wondering if there is a better or more efficient way since the requirement is only to determine if the past 24 months is null/zero or if it has value and does not need to specific value.

Thank you in advance.

Best Regards,

Rafaelle Lim

THANK YOU!
Timo

entry.title: #04 - Launch Your Project: The Broader Perspective Digital Transformation
entry.content:
Transforming your business with cloud technology calls for a paradigm shift in the way you work, think, plan, and execute. This is an exciting and rewarding journey, which requires the right strategy, effort, and investments to get all your people on the same page. Collaboration across different teams and experts is essential to ensure a successful project and to achieve the outcomes you set out for the organization.

A move to the cloud is more than just a software implementation, automating how the business works. It involves changing and adapting core – often long-standing – processes and workflows to meet changing business goals, remain competitive, and meet customer demands. It is about an innovative and business focused approach to redefine how work is done so that it helps the organization succeed. Technology is probably THE most important enabler of the transformation NOT the transformation per se.

The potential of Business Transformation, which implementing any cloud technology brings, will only be determined by establishing a future to present strategy and the efforts being made to drive it.

Enabling Business Transformation

According to the recommendations of many global organizations that already call themselves “successful” on their cloud enabled transformation plus the studies from other reputable sources we say that to enable meaningful Business Transformation results organizations are required to activate the right strategies and programs to accompany their cloud technology implementation and sustainment efforts.

Amazing business results are possible but do not come like magic just because an organization acquires SuccessFactors. “Magic” is the a result of having the right technologies, a future to present mindset and defining the right transformation programs and resources to your organization’s needs. The below Business Transformation Framework highlights the various programs to help with a successful Business Transformation. 

Copyright - Customer Transformation Advisory, SAP SuccessFactors

The Business Transformation Framework encompasses 11 important elements that need to play altogether to achieve expected results:

1. Business Transformation Foundation:

The Foundational elements of the Business Transformation Framework, clarify the purpose of the transformation focusing on the Strategic elements which need to be well defined and communicated. This ensures the direction is well set and all actions should focus on achieving desired outcomes and business goals, which tie up with your overall business strategy and which provide the guiding principles for the way technology is configured and integrated:

1.1. Strategy and Digital Plan:

Establish the future direction and business value focusing on key priorities, goals, and outcomes for the overall business but specially for the business area that is adopting the new technology.  A successful Digital Strategy Plan considers the strategy of the specific business area and then defines where, when and how the use of technology would be essential to drive tangible results.

1.2. Organization and Operating Model:

Define the right Organizational Structure with value-add programs, processes, roles and skills. Does the organization and/or the business area need to revamp itself to deliver value to the internal and external stakeholders? What are the specific skill sets required?  It is best to adopt a “future to present mindset” when designing a model to execute programs and processes for the business with the right people and structure, enabled through a cloud-based solution.

1.3. Stakeholder Experience:

Design the processes to capture the future expectations of your stakeholders what do we want them to feel and sense on their journey?. Focus on the “moments that matter”. Involve your stakeholders in the implementation process earlier on, to help ensure user satisfaction and adoption, and avoid resistance and conflict. To ensure you are continuously reinforcing change and adoption, continue to think from the point of view of your personas and what matters to them.  

2. Operational Excellence:

This refers to the Tactical Programs that need to be in place on a continuous fashion to drive the transformation journey. These define the actions required to achieve your business goals and targets. This set of initiatives should be managed by a core dedicated team and supported by the business defined stakeholders. Let us look at these in detail.

2.1. Executive Sponsorship:

Having an executive sponsor lends credibility to transformation projects and highlights their strategic importance within the organization. It also ensures that resources as needed will be in place and obstacles will be removed or at least reduced at some point. Securing the right sponsor ensures strong leadership support, aids in communicating initiatives, securing necessary resources and budgets, and ensures full organizational buy-in.

2.2. Cloud Culture:

Organizations that embrace a cloud culture focus not only on their technological infrastructure but also on transforming their work processes. They invest in cultivating a new mindset and approach to technology where people is open and prepared to work with technology. To fully leverage the benefits of the cloud, fostering the right attitudes and behaviors among stakeholders is essential. This forms the foundation for successfully achieving business transformation goals.

2.3. Change Management:

The goal of change management is to implement a structured, continuous program that helps people in the organization transition from its current state to a desired future state. This program should focus on people, understanding the target personas, their behaviors, and what motivates them. It should remain relevant even after go-live. A key element is to address the three Vs—Vision, Value, and Vested Interest—of everyone involved and affected by the change through different initiatives as communication, training and sensitization.

According to an SAP Study, employees are more likely to adopt solutions if they help them achieve their goals. The image below illustrates the 3Ws for building trust in technology and encouraging better adoption among your workforce:

2.4. Governance Model:

Designing a program that fosters effective decision-making, ensures organizational alignment, and mitigates risks, should be a priority when designing any transformation project's Governance Model. A strong governance framework is essential for managing technology responsibly and efficiently. The model should begin with a clear purpose statement, include guiding principles, and outline specific goals and outcomes, with defined accountability and ownership for each decision.

Additionally, here’s a sample template of a Model Charter for an Executive Committee within an organization.2.5. Sustainment:

Establish a program to manage and sustain both processes and technology solutions. Technology is never implemented solely to address current challenges; it's always an investment with the future in mind. Sustainment includes planning for ongoing support, adoption strategies, release management, and innovation management. During and after implementation, it's crucial to formally define the roles, responsibilities, and processes required to keep your solution updated, relevant, and continuously delivering business value. A simple RACI matrix can be a good starting point to monitor daily tasks, defining who is Accountable (Owner), Responsible (Doer), Consulted (as needed), and Informed (as required). It is very important to evolve the roles, responsibilities and processes identified during the implementation phase once Live with your solution in order to keep it updated, relevant and producing continued value and desired outcomes.

2.6. Value Management:

Establishing an approach to identify, track, and measure both quantitative and qualitative transformation outcomes is essential. The value management framework focuses on defining the metrics that align with our goals and objectives. It's important to identify and agree on the business KPIs that will drive the most significant outcomes, track them consistently, and measure progress against expectations

2.7. Optimization and Innovation:

This dimension refers to building an approach to advance people, processes, and technology transformation. With Cloud products, innovation is rapid and so should be their adoption. To enable agility under this dimension of the Business Transformation Framework, focus on activities such as evaluating optimization vs innovation within your organization. Innovation introduces new ideas and emerging technology into an existing product, process or service whilst optimization adds incremental improvements to an existing product, process or service. Another focus area should be the ideation of a new strategy which ties in with that of the overall business’ in the future, and with that, make sure to reimagine the programs that are going to be needed to support this new strategy. Conduct a business and functional alignment refresh by ensuring to update common goals, which will serve as the north star for the future direction of current projects and/or sustainment efforts and communicate these transparently. Finally, establish an innovation roadmap by creating a visual representation that aligns the organization’s strategy with innovation actions and brings clarity to the direction, processes, priorities, and timeline required to achieve specific goals. 

2.8. Technology Framework:

Align organizational principles for digital transformation and define tactics to boost adoption and usability for a better experience. The successful implementation of the project would have a lot of technological considerations, like Data Management, aligning with other transformation initiatives within the organization, Technology Roadmap, Network Devices and Mobile functionality. The team members who play a role in implementing the solution, need to make educated and informed decisions. This would mean, the teams to develop strong competencies according to their role, for them to be aware of the Best/ Leading Practices and be able to learning from the proven success of previous implementations and avoiding pitfalls.

3. Technology as the “Enabler”:

Technology will then be the FOUNDATION to support your business strategy Technology will act as both a driver of innovation and a tool for execution. Companies can leverage market leading technology to create digital ecosystems that bring together customers, suppliers, and partners, employee, improving collaboration and creating new value for everyone in the value chain. By aligning technological investments and implementations with business goals, companies can drive innovation, optimize processes, improve customer engagement, and ultimately, create sustainable competitive advantages.

Check out some helpful resources to further aid you with your Business Transformation journey!

  • Learn how organizational change management can contribute to the success of your SAP cloud project through the learning journey Discover Organizational Change Management for SAP Cloud Project and the Change Management Roadmap - Roadmap Viewer (sap.com)
  • Are you implementing any of the SAP SuccessFactors HCM solutions? Register for our Road to Adoption Webcast series to better understand how organizations are making changes to drive adoption and successful business outcomes.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series: Customer Onboarding at SAP
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: React Native app deployment to SAP BTP
entry.content: We are using the CAP Java framework for the deployment.

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud 如何批量上传AR, AP和AA的凭证
entry.content:
在日常业务中，财务人员经常需要批量录入AR, AP以及AA的凭证，但“Upload General Journal Entries"无法使用AR, AP以及AA的分类账。

在S/4HANA Public Cloud中，如何实现批量上传AR, AP和AA的财务凭证？是否有具体的步骤说明？

entry.title: Re: SAP Teched ---- I was worried this year... On-Premise - Favorite Session
entry.content:
Hi Michelle!

I'm in a similar boat given that we are currently still on Netweaver 7.50 for our ERP systems and plan to go to S/4HANA (on prem even if hosted in the cloud) over the next +/- 3 years. So, most of the stuff they talked about during SAP TechEd flew right over my head as I just won't have any means to make use of any of that any time soon!

Having said that, what really piqued my interest is Joule and how it could help with "mundane" development tasks. But, given the often heard caveats that it will only be available for S/4HANA cloud or BTP and the like, it does seem quite out of reach for us as well ☹️. It would really be neat, if Joule could be made available via the ADT regardless of the underlying SAP version in use as that could then possibly help us with needed clean-up tasks of Z-code in the run-up to S/4HANA.

Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: Renaming the title of the Incident in Guided Procedure
entry.content:
Introduction

The following article describes how to change the title in the guided procedure for creating incidents in SAP Solution Manager. This can be done for other guided procedures as well, if they will be used at the customer. The behavior is the same, but you need to change other components in the component workbench.

 

 

 

 

Settings in Component Workbench

Enhancement of the Component

In transaction BSP_WD_CMPWB the component AIC_INCIDENT_G needs to be enhanced.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Choose an existing enhancement set.

 

 

 

In the next screen the name of the application can be choosen by yourself. This needs to be saved in an existing packages created in transaction SE80. The result is, that the component is now enhanced and can be changed.

Enhancement of the MainWindow

In the next step, the main window needs to be enhanced. This can be done with a right click on the MainWindow.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The enhancement of the MainWindow will be stored in a transport request.

Redefine method

The following method must be redefined with right click -> redefine.

IF_BSP_WD_HISTORY_STATE_DESCR~GET_STATE_DESCRIPTION

 

 

 

 

 

The following coding needs to be maintained in this redefined method:

method IF_BSP_WD_HISTORY_STATE_DESCR~GET_STATE_DESCRIPTION.
  description = text-010.
endmethod.

Afterwards the method must be activated.

The program text "text-010" does not exist and this object must be created. This is the title of the Incident. Maintain a new name/title, e.g. <Customer name> Incident creation . The change must be activated again.

 

 

 

 

Result: The title in the Web UI for the Guided Procedure of the Incident is changed.

Conclusion

With these changes you can do some small customer specific entries for IT Service Management, and if you like for Change Request Management or Requirements Management as well. Keep in mind, that you have to find the correct components in the component workbench (e.g. for Change Requests AIC_CMCR_x, Change Documents AIC_CMCD_x).

If you have any questions or input, please feel free to comment and share!

You are also encouraged to follow SAP Solution Manager tag here and the Application Lifecycle Management tag here.

entry.title: ABAP CodeRetreat - Onsite Event in Mannheim -
entry.content:
We are back again with an in Person ABAP Community Events!

We will have fun on the 19th of October 2024 with an ABAP CodeRetreat.

This is a great opportunity to experience a Code Retreat in ABAP and to expand your ABAP-Skills. Spend the day with ABAP-colleagues, connect with your peers, learn from each other, share experiences and be inspired by the great community.

Just bring your own laptop and prepare beforehand your Environment. (See Details on the registration page below)

Please register here! 

*Please note that this event entry is a summarized version of the original event and your registration may be required by the organizer shared on the original event website. RSVP accuracy here is without guarantee and does not constitute registration. However, if you want to earn an SAP Community badge, please leave your RSVP here as well.

entry.title: Re: EML- READ Entity using partial key fields
entry.content:
@Jaison_John 

How about removing the '%key-' and directly specifying the field names like an example below?

READ ENTITY IN LOCAL MODE <Root_entity>

ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( field1 = value1

field2 = value2 ) )

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
HI @David_Chaviano ,

First of all, Thanks a lot for the initiative. I appreciate SAP for the same. 

I would like to join the digital skills initiatives, and I can identify myself as,

  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I am SAP ATTP consultant. I want to shape my career in SAP EWM. I have keen interest in learning warehouse processes and add value in industry. 

I believe, SAP EWM certification will help me in this transition. Kindly share the information

Regards,

Harshendu

entry.title: What's New Viewer Changes
entry.content:
The What's New Viewer feels a bit less helpful than it used to be.

Previously the searches were not calendars.  Instead you got a list of dates, one for each update to the viewer and you could filter on that.  Making it easy to pick up those smaller updates that come following the big one at the start of the release.  And you could see at a glance that were was something.

Now, search on a period and see if there are results.  Its minor I know, but the old way felt more convenient and user friendly.  Or is that just me?

entry.title: Re: EML- READ Entity using partial key fields
entry.content:
Hi Mio,

Unfortunately, that doesn't work because the 3rd key field is still defaulted as '0' and not fetching the result.

entry.title: Re: Chart data label auto change color disabled in design...
entry.content:
Hi,

If I understand your question correctly, are you looking for this color reset button?

Thanks, Jason

entry.title: Service Procurement with spare parts
entry.content:
Dear SAP community,

we have a process that involves external procurement of services (Best Practice 3NI) in a Public Cloud. In addition to the service itself, the supplier is provided with a spare part to use in the service process. The spare part comes from the company that is purchasing the external service from the supplier and is provided free of charge to the supplier.

The spare part is then invoiced to the customer (the place where the service is performed). Do you have any idea how we can handle the spare parts management in this scenario? Best Practice 3NI does not provide any information for this scenario.

Thanks for your input and Best Regards

Sebastian

entry.title: Need to get planning private version using scripting
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a requirement of retrieving planning private version from a dropdown box and display it in table. Whenever I select an item from the dropdown box, only the selected item should be added as version filter in the table and other versions must be removed from table.

Components used: Dropdown box, Table 

I have managed to get private versions to the dropdown box but unable to filter the table.

Code used to get private versions to dropdown box dynamically.

*****************

var list = Table_2.getPlanning().getPrivateVersions();
for(var i=0;i<list.length;i++){
Dropdown_3.addItem(list[i].getDisplayId());
Dropdown_3.setSelectedKey(list[0].getDisplayId());
}
*****************

Any help is appreciated. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Part 2: Posting Documents to SharePoint :Create SharePoint Connector Instance in Open Connectors
entry.content:
Hi,

What are the minimum API permissions required to load SharePoint files into Datasphere?

Regards,

Vivek

entry.title: Termination action - manager redirection
entry.content:
Hello all,

Can anyone help me to configure this?

When a manager is terminated and he has direct reports, SF is stating that there is no event reason.

If termination is done for a normal employee (with no direct reports) it works fine.

entry.title: How to restrict the editing of a form template?
entry.content:
How to limit users' ability to edit the template itself in the user menu, while still allowing them to modify settings, email settings, and select the appropriate default email signatures?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict the editing of a form template?
entry.content:
Hi

Please restrict the access rights of user for work center ID ITS_FORMTEMPLATEMAINT in Administrator work center 

Also refer https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-cloud-for-customer/sap-cloud-for-customer-security-guide/access-restriction

entry.title: Re: GRC 12.0 Integration with S4HANA RISE (Private Cloud)
entry.content: Thank you, Emanuele

entry.title: Have you subscribed to the SAP University Alliances Spotlight?
entry.content:
Subscribe to the quarterly SAP University Alliances Spotlight and learn about the program, teaching resources, collaboration, curricula development, best-practices, (virtual) events, and stay connected with the community in the context of academic teaching related to SAP. 

➡️  https://www.sap.com/cmp/nl/sap-university-alliances-newsletter
